executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with over 100 organizations compromised in a single day on May 14, 2026. High-profile Canadian victims include Canada Goose, National Money Mart (database leaked), Peterborough Public Health, Eastern Townships School Board, Municipality of North Perth, and MRC de Maskinongé. The Qilin ransomware group is the most aggressive actor with 20+ victims, followed by Play, Clop, and Safepay operations. Critical sectors hit include healthcare, education, public sector, financial services, and manufacturing—creating immediate operational risk across Canadian infrastructure.

canada_landscape.summary: Canada experienced a catastrophic ransomware event on May 14, 2026, with over 100 organizations compromised across all provinces and sectors. Critical infrastructure targets include public health agencies (Peterborough Public Health), school boards (Eastern Townships School Board), municipalities (North Perth, MRC de Maskinongé), telecommunications (Eeyou Communications Network), and healthcare providers. No official CCCS advisories were identified in this 48-hour window despite the severity of incidents.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (major apparel retailer) compromised by Coinbasecartel ransomware; Active Green + Ross (automotive/tire retail) hit by Sinobi ransomware
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company (payday loan/financial services) breached by Everest ransomware with database leaked; Morison Insurance Brokers compromised by Sinobi; Trucash (financial services) hit by Lynx; multiple accounting firms including Pewarchuk CPA, MG Chartered Professional Accountant, and Reidhurst Nagy also victimized

report_2w.executive_summary: ShinyHunters has breached Instructure's Canvas learning management system, compromising 275 million student records in what is being called the largest educational data breach in history. The threat actor is conducting a school-by-school extortion campaign. Separately, a critical Linux kernel vulnerability (CVE-2026-31431) dubbed 'Dirty Frag' is under active exploitation, prompting Linux developers to consider an emergency killswitch for vulnerable kernel functions. Multiple critical Cisco ASA/FTD vulnerabilities and a cPanel flaw (CVE-2026-41940) are also being actively exploited in the wild. Canada's cybersecurity agency (CCCS) has issued four advisories in the past two weeks covering these Linux and Cisco threats, plus enterprise SaaS social engineering attacks.

items.description: Linux kernel vulnerability dubbed 'Dirty Frag' enabling root access exploitation
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High
items.access_required: Local access required for privilege escalation

items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: Medium, Availability: Low

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) has issued four alerts in the past two weeks covering critical Linux kernel vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-31431, CVE-2026-43284, CVE-2026-43500), Cisco ASA/FTD device flaws (CVE-2025-20333, CVE-2025-20362, CVE-2025-20363), and social engineering attacks targeting enterprise SaaS environments. A significant breach of Alberta voter data has raised concerns about Canada's electoral integrity, though no specific Canadian retail or financial sector incidents were identified in this reporting period.
